commands.author: clawsweeper[bot]
commands.review_summary:
The branch adds a 150s Codex dynamic-tool timeout for `skill_workshop` plus fake-timer tests around the 90s watchdog and a 120s approval wait.

PR surface: Source +6, Tests +76. Total +82 across 2 files.

Reproducibility: yes. source inspection gives a high-confidence reproduction path: the default Codex watchdog is 90s while Skill Workshop lifecycle approval can wait 120s plus 10s gateway grace. I did not run the live Gateway plus Codex approval flow in this read-only review.
commands.review_followup: - [P2] A narrow automated repair can make the timeout resolver action-aware and cover the sibling side-thread path without a product decision.

target.title: fix(skill-workshop): bound lifecycle approval timeout below Codex dynamic-tool watchdog
target.body:
### Summary

- **Problem**: `skill_workshop` lifecycle tool calls (apply/reject/quarantine) invoked from a Codex agent session fail with "OpenClaw dynamic tool call timed out after 90000ms while running tool skill_workshop" before the operator approval prompt can resolve. The proposal is left pending and unmodified.
- **Root Cause**: The Codex dynamic-tool bridge wraps every tool call with a per-tool watchdog (`CODEX_DYNAMIC_TOOL_TIMEOUT_MS = 90_000`, `dynamic-tool-execution.ts`). Skill Workshop lifecycle calls return a `requireApproval` payload that can wait up to `DEFAULT_PLUGIN_APPROVAL_TIMEOUT_MS = 120_000` plus 10s gateway grace (130s total, `plugin-approvals.ts`, `agent-tools.before-tool-call.ts`). The 90s watchdog fires first, surfacing a generic bridge timeout instead of a clean approval denial; the approval is never completed.
- **Fix**: Add `CODEX_DYNAMIC_SKILL_WORKSHOP_TOOL_TIMEOUT_MS = 150_000` to `readConfiguredDynamicToolTimeoutMs`, matching the existing per-tool deadline pattern used for `message` and `image` tools. The 150s deadline exceeds the 130s approval ceiling, so the approval can now complete or expire cleanly before the Codex watchdog fires. Per-call `timeoutMs` overrides still take precedence. Non-Codex Skill Workshop callers are unaffected.
- **What changed**:
  - `extensions/codex/src/app-server/dynamic-tool-execution.ts` — exported `CODEX_DYNAMIC_SKILL_WORKSHOP_TOOL_TIMEOUT_MS = 150_000` constant; added `skill_workshop` branch in `readConfiguredDynamicToolTimeoutMs`.
  - `extensions/codex/src/app-server/dynamic-tool-execution.test.ts` — three new tests: resolver returns the constant for `skill_workshop`; fake-timer proof that the 90s watchdog fires before a 120s approval (before); fake-timer proof that the 120s approval completes within the 150s deadline (after).
- **What did NOT change (scope boundary)**:
  - Skill Workshop approval policy (`src/skills/workshop/policy.ts`) is unchanged; the 120s approval window is preserved for all callers.
  - Config surface unchanged (no schema, defaults, doctor migrations, or `docs/reference/config`).
  - Plugin surface unchanged (no plugin SDK, manifest, `extensions/api.ts`, registry/loader).
  - Gateway protocol unchanged.

### Reproduction

1. Configure Skill Workshop with default `approvalPolicy: pending`.
2. Start a Codex agent session and prompt it to apply a pending skill proposal.
3. The agent invokes `skill_workshop(action="apply", proposal_id="...")`.
4. **Before this PR**: the Codex dynamic-tool watchdog fires at 90s with "OpenClaw dynamic tool call timed out after 90000ms while running tool skill_workshop"; the approval prompt is never shown; the proposal stays pending.
5. **After this PR**: the 150s Codex deadline lets the approval flow reach its natural 120s expiry; if the operator does not respond the approval times out with a clean denial; if the operator responds the apply proceeds normally.

### Real behavior proof

**Behavior addressed (#91266)**: `skill_workshop(action="apply")` from a Codex agent session no longer surfaces a generic 90s dynamic-tool timeout; the 150s extended deadline gives the approval path (up to 130s total) room to resolve.

**Real environment tested (Linux, Node 22 — Vitest with fake timers against `handleDynamicToolCallWithTimeout` and `resolveDynamicToolCallTimeoutMs`, the two production functions on the timed-out path)**: fake-timer tests drive `handleDynamicToolCallWithTimeout` directly with a mock `toolBridge` that simulates the approval wait duration, confirming the watchdog fires at 90s before the approval can complete and that the same 120s approval succeeds within the 150s deadline.

**Exact steps or command run after this patch**: `node scripts/run-vitest.mjs extensions/codex/src/app-server/dynamic-tool-execution.test.ts`

**Evidence after fix**:

```text
 Test Files  1 passed (1)
      Tests  15 passed (15)
```

Three new tests: (1) "uses an extended deadline for skill_workshop lifecycle tool calls" — `resolveDynamicToolCallTimeoutMs` returns `CODEX_DYNAMIC_SKILL_WORKSHOP_TOOL_TIMEOUT_MS` (150s). (2) "fires the 90s watchdog before a skill_workshop lifecycle approval can resolve" — `handleDynamicToolCallWithTimeout` at `timeoutMs: 90_000` with a never-resolving bridge mock fails with the exact timeout message at 90s; this is the before state. (3) "lets a skill_workshop lifecycle approval complete within the extended 150s deadline" — `handleDynamicToolCallWithTimeout` at `timeoutMs: 150_000` with a mock that resolves after 120s (DEFAULT_PLUGIN_APPROVAL_TIMEOUT_MS) succeeds; this is the after state.

**Observed result after fix**: the before test confirms the 90s watchdog fires while the approval is still pending; the after test confirms a full 120s approval wait completes successfully within the 150s window; both use the same `handleDynamicToolCallWithTimeout` entry point that Codex reaches in production.

**What was not tested**: live Gateway + Codex agent session with an active operator approval prompt in the UI.

**Repro confirmation**: the before test fails if `timeoutMs` is raised to 150_000 (approval would complete); the after test fails if `timeoutMs` is lowered to 90_000 (watchdog fires at 90s before the 120s approval). The two tests together pin the exact boundary the fix crosses.

### Risk / Mitigation

- **Risk**: `skill_workshop` non-lifecycle calls (list, inspect) also receive the 150s Codex deadline instead of the 90s default. **Mitigation**: non-lifecycle calls complete in milliseconds and are not affected by the wider window; per-call `timeoutMs` can still narrow this for any call.

### Change Type (select all)

- [x] Bug fix

### Scope (select all touched areas)

- [x] Agent runtime
- [x] Codex extension

### Linked Issue/PR

Fixes #91266
target.author: openperf
